sanskrit:
योऽन्तःसुखोऽन्तरारामस्तथान्तर्ज्योतिरेव यः।
स योगी ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं ब्रह्मभूतोऽधिगच्छति॥
transliteration:
yo ’ntaḥ-sukho ’ntar-ārāmas tathāntar-jyotir eva yaḥ |
sa yogī brahma-nirvāṇaṁ brahma-bhūto ’dhigacchati ||
literal_meaning: One whose happiness is within, who delights within, whose light/aim is within—such a yogī, becoming Brahman, attains Brahmanirvāṇa.
contextual_meaning: True psychological freedom is an inside job: when affect, motivation, and meaning are self-sourced rather than stimulus-bound, the mind ceases to project deficits outward and abides in its own innate fullness.
psychological_state: Autonomous, self-luminous awareness; reduced need for external reinforcement; congruence between inner experience and outer action.
trigger_scenario: After a week-long digital detox you notice you can sit quietly without FOMO; your mood no longer rises and falls with notifications, likes, or shopping.
anchor_text: Find joy inside yourself; when your light shines within, you are already free.
